'''Hirsi''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] on the [[Cerean Reach/Legends|Cerean Reach]] in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].

==Description==
[[File:Qwohog8.jpg|250px|left|thumb|A Qwohog swimming on Hirsi]]
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Hirsi was an aquatic world, with much of the surface covered by freshwater lakes, in addition to streams and rivers, which were inhabited by the native [[Qwohog (species)|Qwohog]] [[Species/Legends|species]], as well as landmasses containing traces of [[rudic crystal]]s. Qwohog could detect the scents and toxic substances in the water. [[Basin-pod]]s grew on the surface, and the waters were home to [[tenidit plant]]s and [[Moss/Legends|moss]]. [[Skitterfly/Legends|skitterflies]] and varieties of [[Fish/Legends|fish]] lived in the lakes, including [[Shellfish/Legends|shellfish]].<ref name="GG12" />

==History==
The native Qwohog, the planet's only [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]] species, lived in communities in almost all of the planet's large lakes, farming freshwater plants and moss. They sent hunting parties ashore to capture lizards and tiny mammals for food, fashioned [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] from tenidit plants, and used shellfish in medicines. Each community was led by a ruling council, with members elected from exclusive highborn families.<ref name="GG12" />

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], the planet environment was devastated. The planet was subjugated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] for its [[rudic crystal]] mines during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="GG12" />

The planet was later destroyed by the resurgent Empire's [[Galaxy Gun]] during [[Operation Shadow Hand]].<ref name="TEA" />

{{Planet-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]'', a ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement first published on [[April 19]], [[2003]],<ref name="UAA" /> and the [[December 9]], [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="CSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> place Hirsi in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], whereas the [[August 18]], [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places it in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="TEA" />
